data.content:
### Install Mailpit via Script (Linux & Mac)

Source: https://github.com/axllent/mailpit/blob/develop/README.md

Commands to install Mailpit directly to a specified path (defaulting to /usr/local/bin/mailpit) using a curl-based installation script. An option to change the installation path via an environment variable is also provided.

```shell
sudo sh < <(curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/axllent/mailpit/develop/install.sh)
```

```shell
INSTALL_PATH=/usr/bin sudo sh < <(curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/axllent/mailpit/develop/install.sh)
```

--------------------------------

### Mailpit General Usage Commands

Source: https://github.com/axllent/mailpit/blob/develop/README.md

Basic commands for interacting with Mailpit, including displaying help options and starting the service if installed via Homebrew.

```shell
mailpit -h
```

```shell
brew services start mailpit
```

--------------------------------

### Install Mailpit via Package Managers

Source: https://github.com/axllent/mailpit/blob/develop/README.md

Commands to install Mailpit using common package managers like Homebrew for macOS and pkg for FreeBSD. This includes the initial installation and starting Mailpit as a background service.

```shell
brew install mailpit
```

```shell
brew services start mailpit
```

```shell
pkg install mailpit
```

--------------------------------

### Clone Your Forked Mailpit Repository

Source: https://github.com/axllent/mailpit/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md

This snippet provides the bash commands necessary to clone your forked Mailpit repository from GitHub to your local machine and then navigate into the newly created project directory.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/your-username/mailpit.git
cd mailpit
```

--------------------------------

### Commit and Push Changes to Remote Repository

Source: https://github.com/axllent/mailpit/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md

These bash commands are used to stage all local changes, commit them with a clear and concise message, and then push the newly created branch to your remote GitHub repository, making your changes available for a pull request.

```bash
git add .
git commit -m "Describe your changes"
git push origin feature/your-feature-name
```

--------------------------------

### Create a New Git Branch for Features

Source: https://github.com/axllent/mailpit/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md

This command demonstrates how to create a new Git branch with a descriptive name. It's crucial for isolating your changes for a specific feature or bug fix, ensuring a clean development workflow.

```bash
git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name
```

=== COMPLETE CONTENT === This response contains all available snippets from this library. No additional content exists. Do not make further requests.
